heading: Program of payments to teaching health centers that operate graduate medical education programs
label: 256h Program of payments to teaching health centers that operate graduate medical education programs

Funding for Teaching Health Centers That Operate Graduate Medical Education, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–2, title II, § 2604135 Stat. 44Mar. 11, 2021“(a).—In GeneralIn addition to amounts otherwise available, and notwithstanding the capped amount referenced in sections 340H(b)(2) and 340H(d)(2) of the Public Health Service Act ( and (d)(2)), there is appropriated to the Secretary [of Health and Human Services] for fiscal year 2021, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $330,000,000, to remain available until , for the program of payments to teaching health centers that operate graduate medical education under section 340H of the Public Health Service Act () and for teaching health center development grants authorized under section 749A of the Public Health Service Act (–1).42 U.S.C. 256h(b)(2)42 U.S.C. 256h42 U.S.C. 293September 30, 2023l“(b).—Use of FundsAmounts made available pursuant to subsection (a) shall be used for the following activities:“(1)For making payments to establish new approved graduate medical residency training programs pursuant to section 340H(a)(1)(C) of the Public Health Service Act ().42 U.S.C. 256h(a)(1)(C)“(2)To provide an increase to the per resident amount described in section 340H(a)(2) of the Public Health Service Act () of $10,000.42 U.S.C. 256h(a)(2)“(3)For making payments under section 340H(a)(1)(A) of the Public Health Service Act ()) [sic] to qualified teaching health centers for maintenance of filled positions at existing approved graduate medical residency training programs.42 U.S.C. 256h(a)(1)(A)“(4)For making payments under section 340H(a)(1)(B) of the Public Health Service Act () for the expansion of existing approved graduate medical residency training programs.42 U.S.C. 256h(a)(1)(B)“(5)For making awards under section 749A of the Public Health Service Act (–1) to teaching health centers for the purpose of establishing new accredited or expanded primary care residency programs.42 U.S.C. 293l“(6)To cover administrative costs and activities necessary for qualified teaching health centers receiving payments under section 340H of the Public Health Service Act () to carry out activities under such section.”42 U.S.C. 256h

children.label: A
children.text: maintenance of filled positions at existing approved graduate medical residency training programs;

children.label: B
children.text: expansion of existing approved graduate medical residency training programs; and

children.label: C
children.text: establishment of new approved graduate medical residency training programs.

children.heading: Per resident amount
children.label: 2 Per resident amount
children.text: In making payments under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall consider the cost of training residents at teaching health centers and the implications of the per resident amount on approved graduate medical residency training programs at teaching health centers.

children.label: A
children.text: serve a health professional shortage area with a designation in effect under or a medically underserved community (as defined in ); orsection 254e of this titlesection 295p of this title

children.label: B
children.text: are located in a rural area (as defined in ).section 1395ww(d)(2)(D) of this title

children.heading: Direct expense amount
children.label: A Direct expense amount
children.text: The amount determined under subsection (c) for direct expenses associated with sponsoring approved graduate medical residency training programs.

children.heading: Indirect expense amount
children.label: B Indirect expense amount
children.text: The amount determined under subsection (d) for indirect expenses associated with the additional costs relating to teaching residents in such programs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The total of the payments made to qualified teaching health centers under paragraph (1)(A) or paragraph (1)(B) in a fiscal year shall not exceed the amount of funds appropriated under subsection (g) for such payments for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall limit the funding of full-time equivalent residents in order to ensure the direct and indirect payments as determined under subsection (c) and (d) do not exceed the total amount of funds appropriated in a fiscal year under subsection (g).1So in original. Probably should be “subsections”.1

children.heading: Addition
children.label: C Addition
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of this section, for the period beginning on , and ending on , the Secretary may use any amounts made available in any fiscal year to carry out this section (including amounts recouped under subsection (f)) to make payments described in paragraphs (1)(A) and (1)(B), in addition to the total amount of funds appropriated under subsection (g).October 1, 2023December 31, 2024

children.heading: Amount of payment for direct graduate medical education
children.label: c Amount of payment for direct graduate medical education
A
children.text: the updated national per resident amount for direct graduate medical education, as determined under paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: the average number of full-time equivalent residents in the teaching health center’s graduate approved medical residency training programs as determined under (without regard to the limitation under subparagraph (F) of such section) during the fiscal year.section 1395ww(h)(4) of this title

children.heading: Updated national per resident amount for direct graduate medical education
children.label: 2 Updated national per resident amount for direct graduate medical education

children.heading: Determination of qualified teaching health center per resident amount
children.label: A Determination of qualified teaching health center per resident amount
i
children.text: by dividing the national average per resident amount computed under into a wage-related portion and a non-wage related portion by applying the proportion determined under subparagraph (B);section 256e(c)(2)(D) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: by multiplying the wage-related portion by the factor applied under (but without application of section 4410 of the Balanced Budget Act of 1997 ( note)) during the preceding fiscal year for the teaching health center’s area; andsection 1395ww(d)(3)(E) of this title42 U.S.C. 1395ww

children.label: iii
children.text: by adding the non-wage-related portion to the amount computed under clause (ii).

children.heading: Updating rate
children.label: B Updating rate
children.text: The Secretary shall update such per resident amount for each such qualified teaching health center as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Amount of payment for indirect medical education
children.label: d Amount of payment for indirect medical education

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount determined under this subsection for payments to qualified teaching health centers for indirect expenses associated with the additional costs of teaching residents for a fiscal year is equal to an amount determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: evaluate indirect training costs relative to supporting a primary care residency program in qualified teaching health centers; and

children.label: B
children.text: based on this evaluation, assure that the aggregate of the payments for indirect expenses under this section and the payments for direct graduate medical education as determined under subsection (c) in a fiscal year do not exceed the amount appropriated for such expenses as determined in subsection (g).

children.heading: Interim payment
children.label: 3 Interim payment
children.text: Before the Secretary makes a payment under this subsection pursuant to a determination of indirect expenses under paragraph (1), the Secretary may provide to qualified teaching health centers a payment, in addition to any payment made under subsection (c), for expected indirect expenses associated with the additional costs of teaching residents for a fiscal year, based on an estimate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Clarification regarding relationship to other payments for graduate medical education
children.label: e Clarification regarding relationship to other payments for graduate medical education
A
children.text: for the indirect costs of medical education under ;section 1395ww(d)(5)(B) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: for direct graduate medical education costs under ; andsection 1395ww(h) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: for direct costs of medical education under ;section 1395ww(k) of this title

shall not be taken into account in applying the limitation on the number of total full-time equivalent residents under subparagraphs (F) and (G) of and clauses (v), (vi)(I), and (vi)(II) of for the portion of time that a resident rotates to a hospital; andsection 1395ww(h)(4) of this titlesection 1395ww(d)(5)(B) of this title

shall not include the time in which a resident is counted toward full-time equivalency by a hospital under paragraph (2) or under , , or .section 1395ww(d)(5)(B)(iv) of this titlesection 1395ww(h)(4)(E) of this titlesection 256e of this title

children.heading: Reconciliation
children.label: f Reconciliation
children.text: The Secretary shall determine any changes to the number of residents reported by a teaching health center in the application of the teaching health center for the current fiscal year to determine the final amount payable to the teaching health center for the current fiscal year for both direct expense and indirect expense amounts. Based on such determination, the Secretary shall recoup any overpayments made to pay any balance due to the extent possible. The final amount so determined shall be considered a final intermediary determination for the purposes of section 1395 of this title and shall be subject to administrative and judicial review under that section in the same manner as the amount of payment under section 1395ww(d) of this title is subject to review under such section.oo2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: A
children.text: $230,000,000, for the period of fiscal years 2011 through 2015;

children.label: B
children.text: $60,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2016 and 2017;

children.label: C
children.text: $126,500,000 for each of fiscal years 2018 through 2023;

children.label: D
children.text: $168,915,878 for fiscal year 2024;

children.label: E
children.text: $181,563,574 for fiscal year 2025;

children.label: F
children.text: $225,000,000 for fiscal year 2026;

children.label: G
children.text: $250,000,000 for fiscal year 2027;

children.label: H
children.text: $275,000,000 for fiscal year 2028; and

children.label: I
children.text: $300,000,000 for fiscal year 2029.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: 2 Administrative expenses
children.text: Of the amount made available to carry out this section for any fiscal year, the Secretary may not use more than 5 percent of such amount for the expenses of administering this section.

children.label: A
children.text: The types of primary care resident approved training programs that the qualified teaching health center provided for residents.

children.label: B
children.text: The number of approved training positions for residents described in paragraph (4).

children.label: C
children.text: The number of residents described in paragraph (4) who completed their residency training at the end of such residency academic year and care for vulnerable populations living in underserved areas.

children.label: D
children.text: The number of patients treated by residents described in paragraph (4).

children.label: E
children.text: The number of visits by patients treated by residents described in paragraph (4).

children.label: F
children.text: Of the number of residents described in paragraph (4) who completed their residency training at the end of such residency academic year, the number and percentage of such residents entering primary care practice (meaning any of the areas of practice listed in the definition of a primary care residency program in section 293–1 of this title).l

children.label: i
children.text: primarily serving a health professional shortage area with a designation in effect under or a medically underserved community (as defined in ); orsection 254e of this titlesection 295p of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: located in a rural area (as defined in ).section 1395ww(d)(2)(D) of this title

children.label: H
children.text: Other information as deemed appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Audit authority
children.label: A Audit authority
children.text: The Secretary may audit a qualified teaching health center to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the information submitted in a report under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Limitation on payment
children.label: B Limitation on payment
children.text: A teaching health center may only receive payment in a cost reporting period for a number of such resident positions that is greater than the base level of primary care resident positions, as determined by the Secretary. For purposes of this subparagraph, the “base level of primary care residents” for a teaching health center is the level of such residents as of a base period.

children.heading: Reduction in payment for failure to report
children.label: 3 Reduction in payment for failure to report
i
children.text: the qualified teaching health center has failed to provide the Secretary, as an addendum to the qualified teaching health center’s application under this section for such fiscal year, the report required under paragraph (1) for the previous fiscal year; or

children.label: ii
children.text: such report fails to provide complete and accurate information required under any subparagraph of such paragraph.

children.heading: Notice and opportunity to provide accurate and missing information
children.label: B Notice and opportunity to provide accurate and missing information
children.text: Before imposing a reduction under subparagraph (A) on the basis of a qualified teaching health center’s failure to provide complete and accurate information described in subparagraph (A)(ii), the Secretary shall provide notice to the teaching health center of such failure and the Secretary’s intention to impose such reduction and shall provide the teaching health center with the opportunity to provide the required information within the period of 30 days beginning on the date of such notice. If the teaching health center provides such information within such period, no reduction shall be made under subparagraph (A) on the basis of the previous failure to provide such information.

children.heading: Residents
children.label: 4 Residents
children.text: The residents described in this paragraph are those who are in part-time or full-time equivalent resident training positions at a qualified teaching health center in any approved graduate medical residency training program.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: i Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate regulations to carry out this section.

children.heading: Approved graduate medical residency training program
children.label: 1 Approved graduate medical residency training program
A
children.text: participation in which may be counted toward certification in a specialty or subspecialty and includes formal postgraduate training programs in geriatric medicine approved by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: that meets criteria for accreditation (as established by the Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education, the American Osteopathic Association, or the American Dental Association).

children.heading: New approved graduate medical residency training program
children.label: 2 New approved graduate medical residency training program
children.text: The term “new approved graduate medical residency training program” means an approved graduate medical residency training program for which the sponsoring qualified teaching health center has not received a payment under this section for a previous fiscal year (other than pursuant to subsection (a)(1)(C)).

children.heading: Primary care residency program
children.label: 3 Primary care residency program
children.text: The term “primary care residency program” has the meaning given that term in section 293–1 of this title.l

children.heading: Qualified teaching health center
children.label: 4 Qualified teaching health center
children.text: The term “qualified teaching health center” has the meaning given the term “teaching health center” in section 293–1 of this title.l
